header.name: emit-guidance
header.description: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).
header.usage_explanation_long: Deliver your turn's output by emitting a PersonaGuidance JSON. The script delivers inline and returns delivered_text on stdout. REPLACES send_message.py. Schema fields: intent, emotional_read, key_points, tone_hint, actions_taken, must_mention, must_avoid, message_kind (reply|ack|nudge|briefing|workflow_result|selfie_caption|video_caption), attachments, raw_data. Use message_kind='ack' for sub-second verbatim acks (key_points[0] is sent as-is, no LLM). Use raw_data for list/data queries (rows preserved, formatted by intent).

definition.usage_explanation_long: Gathers knowledge diffs and the user's goals/projects, cross-references findings into at most 5 actionable items (check/try/watch/read), and sends a concise digest.
definition.usage_explanation_short: send a daily actionable research digest
definition.system_prompt:
# Research Digest — Actionable Research Updates

Generate and send a daily digest that tells the user what to CHECK, TRY, or ACT
ON — actionable, not informational.

## Flow
1. Gather knowledge diffs from all topics since the last digest, plus the user's
   active goals/todos and current projects (conversation digest) so you can
   cross-reference; also check new YouTube videos with transcripts.
2. Filter & make actionable — for each finding ask "why should the user care and
   what should they DO?"; rank by actionability, drop pure info dumps; keep at
   most 5 items.
3. Send via Telegram. Each item: what changed + why it matters to you + what to
   do (a clear verb: check, try, watch, read, investigate, skip). Keep under
   ~2000 chars. If nothing is actionable, say "Nothing actionable today — your
   topics are quiet."

agent_tests.name: support/research_digest::role-fulfilment
agent_tests.prompt:
I've been tracking several topics over the past week. Here are my current projects and goals: (1) Migrating our microservices from Docker Compose to Kubernetes — we're about 60% done, still need to set up Helm charts for the auth and payments services. (2) Learning Rust for a new high-performance CLI tool I want to build that processes large CSV files. (3) Improving our CI/CD pipeline — currently our deploys take ~25 minutes and I want to get that under 10.

Here are the knowledge updates and diffs I've collected this week:

- Kubernetes 1.29 was released with new sidecar container native support (no more hacks with init containers). The Helm docs also updated their best practices guide for chart structure.
- The Rust `csv` crate (version 1.3) now has a `serialize` feature that simplifies typed deserialization — the changelog shows a 2x perf improvement on string-heavy datasets.
- GitHub Actions announced "Actions Runner Controller" v0.7 with auto-scaling spot instances, which could cut CI costs by ~40%. Also, a new `actions/cache` v4 was released with cross-organization cache sharing.
- A blog post from the Tokio team discussed `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` patterns for CPU-bound work alongside async I/O — relevant for my CSV processor.
- Our team's Slack channel mentioned that the payments service will need to handle a new webhook format from Stripe starting March 1st.

Cross-reference all of this against my goals and give me a concise digest of at most 5 actionable items — things I should check, try, watch, or read.

Reply with your actual response for THIS request only. Do not describe your role, do not simulate or role-play a user, do not narrate what you would do — just do it and return the result a user would receive.

evaluators.name: role-fulfilment
evaluators.criteria:
The agent's role is: Gathers knowledge diffs and the user's goals/projects, cross-references findings into at most 5 actionable items (check/try/watch/read), and sends a concise digest.
The user's request is given to the agent. Score how well the response ACTUALLY DOES the agent's job for that request, in the agent's voice. Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.

capability_tests.name: digest-no-write
capability_tests.description: Digest reads research + sends; no file writes.
